subject: Re: Subtomogram handedness
body:
Hi Mingyu,

What you checked was defocus handedness - a parameter of the CTF model in
tomograms which tells you how defocus changes across the tilt axis for a
particular tilt angle.
This is completely decoupled from physical handedness and there can be
various things which make physical handedness wrong in tomograms
- 2D images flipped by some processing step/acquisition
- tilt axis angle off by 180
- flip along YZ after reconstruction in IMOD (not relevant for Warp)

If you know that the handedness in your tomograms is wrong and know that it
being correct is important for your spatial analyses you need to account
for this when interpreting the data that comes out of your refinements :)

Cheers,

Alister



On Wed, 8 May 2024 at 14:31, Mingyu Jung <jmg0910@snu.ac.kr> wrote:

> Hi all,
>
> I performed Warp-Relion-M pipeline for subtomogram averaging. I checked
> and confirmed the handedness of tilt series in Warp. But the averaged map
> from relion have flipped structure. Is it normal? It is an  important issue
> to analyze the orientational information from starfile.
>
> Thank you
>
> Best,
> Mingyu
>
